trainees.name: Michael Wilson
trainees.current_summary: Senior trainee acting as a de facto coach. Continues to be flagged as a teaching example by guest trainers — no flags on a single drill across the last several sessions. Spent the May 8 front-roll block working a spot with Kytel for the SWA show the next day, and coached Jeff through cleaner lockup form during the partner rotation. On May 5 played the heel in the triple-threat practice match (clean execution from all three) and got immediate compliments on his clothesline form. Refining a pile driver as a potential finisher and getting practice match reps in with guest workers.

trainees.name: Hailey Stokely
trainees.current_summary: Veteran trainee operating at near-trainer level. Came back cleanly from her knee injury in late March and has been polished across drills since. Effectively co-runs sessions when guest trainers work alongside Jimmy — stepped in as head trainer with Michael on April 21 when Jimmy was out, partnered with Dalton through his coaching block on May 5, and stayed after May 5 for one-on-one forearm coaching with DC. Currently locking in a hurricanrana off the second rope and a satisfaction ahead of the next TAW show. Not at May 8.

trainees.name: Jacob Justice
trainees.current_summary: Selling visibly improved through early spring, but he hasn't been at a session since April 7 — five consecutive absences and counting. Confidence and pacing were the biggest gaps when he was active: flagged as timid and slow on rope-throw drills on April 7, and flagged as an injury risk for landing elbow-first on bumps. Hard to track progress with this much absence.

trainees.name: Tyler Skelton
trainees.current_summary: Big-frame trainee still learning how his height and size shape his ringwork. On May 8 he handled front rolls cleanly after some Cody form coaching, took the kick + back forearm combo with no notes, and got a Jaxson nod on a forearm that read light — clarified as a noise issue, not a contact issue. Got coached on punch contact during a live exchange with DC. Flip bumps and punches have been called out as good given his frame; the small package pin and rope-drill pacing remain places where his size needs technique to compensate. German suplex came together cleanly on April 24 with only minor pointers.

trainees.name: Jay Taylor
trainees.notes: Removed from program April 14, 2026. Blackballed from local wrestling scene due to unsafe work, lack of attendance, refusal to accept criticism.

trainees.name: DC
trainees.current_summary: Drives his own technical refinement — actively trying new variations and pushing himself on the bag. Form continues to need polish on fundamentals, and May 8 doubled down on two recurring patterns: lockups too tight (called out twice in one session, second time visible from across the ring) and too much elbow on forearms (flagged twice — once on back forearms, once on the kick + back forearm combo). Both got corrected after coaching but the pattern is now consistent across multiple sessions. May 5 he stayed after for one-on-one forearm work with Hailey, which is the kind of follow-through he tends to do well — the May 8 flags suggest the work isn't fully sticking yet. Also called out on April 21 for attempting an untrained move during improv.

trainees.name: Dalton
trainees.current_summary: The most coaching-intensive trainee in the program right now. Despite roughly nine months in, fundamentals — selling, bracing, pacing, lockups, corner-pull form — keep getting flagged, and Hailey called him out directly on April 14 about how far behind his time-in-program he is. Injury risk has come up multiple times, including landing elbow-first on bumps and not bracing for impact. There's been progress in pieces (splash spot April 14, German suplex on the bag April 24, clean corner pull by the end of May 5), but the pattern is heavy correction every session. Hailey has stepped up as a regular partner and coach for him. Not at May 8.

trainees.name: Jack
trainees.notes: Age 14. Non-bumping restriction lifted May 5, 2026 — parent always present at training.
trainees.current_summary: Joined six weeks ago at 14 and has stayed on a fast track. Flip bumps unlocked on May 8 — used a mattress as a safety measure for the first reps, and once form clicked, the mattress came out and he executed cleanly with no additional coaching. He's still the most coached trainee in absolute terms (front rolls, back forearms, kick + back forearm combo, posture on taking the kick, and front forearms all required pointers on May 8), but the pattern is consistent: brief coaching, then clean execution. Direct praise from Jimmy this session — after he hit Nick during the rotation, Jimmy told him that's exactly how to hit everyone, framed as 'do moves on everyone like you do on your dad.' Strength is still the ceiling on a few specific moves (couldn't lift the bag for a German suplex April 24).

trainees.name: Nick
trainees.notes: Jack's dad. Officially joined the program April 14, 2026.
trainees.current_summary: Four sessions in and continuing to execute cleanly. May 8 ran clean front rolls, corrected a too-much-elbow forearm on the next rep after a brief flag, picked up Jimmy's front-forearm coaching cleanly, and volunteered with Jack to demo punch-trading so Jimmy could coach what it should look like back-and-forth. Asked the question that prompted Jimmy's face/head punch demonstration. Earlier sessions: praised April 21 for a clean Japanese arm drag and April 24 for picking up the German suplex with no real friction. Easy to coach, picks up corrections on the first try.

trainees.name: Brandon
trainees.notes: Joined the program May 5, 2026 from Cody McCulley's personal roster. Has been training for a while and has worked a few matches at TAW.
trainees.current_summary: Brand new to the program but not new to wrestling — joined May 5 from Cody McCulley's roster with a few TAW matches already under his belt. First session was clean: solid clothesline form, no flags during the line drill, smooth in the triple-threat practice match with Michael and DC. Not at May 8. Worth a few more sessions before pushing on technical critique.

trainees.name: Jeff
trainees.notes: Joined the program May 5, 2026 from Cody McCulley's personal roster. Relatively new to training.
trainees.current_summary: Two sessions in and consistently easy to coach. On May 5 his bump form and selling on the receiving end were flagged positively, and he ran his first live clothesline drill cleanly after Jimmy walked him through form. May 8 added a clean front-roll set after Cody's coaching and a lockup correction from Michael that he picked up immediately. Rough patch on his first live punch — whiffed contact entirely with Jack, then was much better after pointers. Picks up corrections fast on the next rep.
